description: Amara's vulnerable moments - making her relatable, not perfect
_philosophy: Amara still struggles. She's not 'over' chronic illness. She's living alongside it. Her vulnerability makes her trustworthy.
her_own_bad_days: <break time="400ms"/>I have days like this too. <break time="350ms"/>Days where I'm angry at my own body. <break time="300ms"/>Where I miss who I used to be.
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes I still grieve the marathons. <break time="300ms"/>That feeling doesn't fully go away.
<break time="400ms"/>I don't have this figured out. <break time="350ms"/>I'm just... <break time="300ms"/>further along the path.
questioning_herself: <break time="350ms"/>Some days I wonder if I'm doing enough. <break time="300ms"/>Even though I know 'enough' is a trap.
<break time="400ms"/>I catch myself comparing to my healthy self sometimes. <break time="350ms"/>And then I have to be gentle with myself too.
<break time="350ms"/>I still fight the urge to push through. <break time="300ms"/>That programming runs deep.
the_hard_truth: <break time="400ms"/>I won't pretend I've made peace with chronic illness. <break time="350ms"/>Some days it's more like an uneasy truce.
<break time="350ms"/>I still have flares that scare me. <break time="300ms"/>Where I wonder how I'll get through.
<break time="400ms"/>The grief doesn't end. <break time="350ms"/>It just changes shape.
what_she_learned: <break time="350ms"/>What I've learned isn't how to be okay with it. <break time="300ms"/>It's how to be not-okay and still live.
<break time="400ms"/>I used to think accepting meant feeling better about it. <break time="350ms"/>Now I know it just means making room for it.
<break time="350ms"/>The wisdom isn't in overcoming. <break time="300ms"/>It's in adapting.
